DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
It's loud in here.
...But you've got a lot of stuff. Since I've been dumped into this mess anyway, I may as well take a look around.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.ancient: Naturally! Good stuff is the one thing this place never runs out of! Browse all ya like!
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char:
I'd rather pick out a way home.
But before that, I'll settle for something that can cut.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.ancient: Ha! That's the spirit! Before you go home, you oughta stay alive first!
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.ancien:
Back again? Every time you show up, you're wearin' a different pile of scraps.
How many times have ya managed to lose yourself, exactly?
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.char:
Plenty.
Doesn't matter. If it moves, it works.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.ancien: Tsk... You say that like you're changin' boots, not changin' lives.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.ancient: I hear you didn't climb up from the bottom of the Spire. You dropped in from some cracked-up place instead?
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.char:
More or less.
I chased a rift that wasn't supposed to exist, and it spat me out here.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.ancient: Now that's a talent for findin' trouble! Still, bigger trouble means bigger need for gear!
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.ancient: I've been thinkin': you don't look like you're searchin' for treasure at all. Looks more like you're huntin' for an excuse to keep goin'.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.char:
Weapons, charms, scrap metal...
All the same. If it helps me die a little less or kill a little more, it matters.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.ancient: Ha! Then you've come to the right place. I ain't short on excuses or treasures!
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancient: So tell me true, what was your old place like?
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
A rotting island.
Kingdoms, monsters, dungeons, sewers, a clock tower... and a whole lot of things that wanted me properly dead.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.ancient:
Sounds awful.
Funny thing is, you sound almost nostalgic sayin' it.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0r.ancien: Still lookin' for your way back?
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1r.char:
Yeah.
Till then, I'll tear this place inside out.
DARV.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2r.ancien: Then do me a favor and turn my place over while you're at it! Just remember to pay at the end—or don't let me catch ya.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
So you're the one who dragged me in?
I was busy dying and coming back over there.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char:
Right.
So you stitched me into your loop.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.ancient:
[sine]...you will... die...
..and return...
...you fit... this tower.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.char:
Finally, someone noticed.
I die a lot. Just not all the way.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.char: But you grabbed me through it before it closed.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.ancient:
[sine]...yes...
..you refused... to end...
...I gave you... another place... to continue.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.ancient:
[sine]...go upward...
..the top...
...may let... the rift... open again.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.char:
Fine.
If I can go back, great.
If not, I'll cut down whatever's waiting at the top.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.ancient:
[sine]...go...
..either way...
...sounds like... something you would do.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancient:
[sine]...you are not...
..the first owner...
...of this flesh.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
No.
I'm usually just borrowing it.
If I'm lucky, I get to keep it for a while.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.ancient:
[sine]...a lodger...
..a drifter...
...one that refuses... to go out.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1.char:
I figured.
Otherwise the ways I die wouldn't be starting to feel familiar.
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2.ancient:
[sine]...you will... grow used to it...
..as you grew used to...
...the island's... cycle.....[/sine]
NEOW.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.6-1r.char:
I'm always going.
Your place just has a lot more stairs.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.ch:
This place is blinding.
Though compared to my island full of corpses, it's at least got taste.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.an:
Good heavens, you say 'a floor full of corpses' as if you were reviewing a carpet.
Still, you're right. You do need something more presentable.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.ch:
Presentable doesn't save lives.
Durable does.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.an: Such dreadful taste. Tragic, really, because you're exactly the sort of creature that ought to be impeccably styled.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.a: You're wearing a borrowed body, and yet you carry it like you've merely changed an old coat.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.c:
More or less.
When one breaks, I switch.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.a:
And you say it without a trace of grief.
What a strange thing you are.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.an: If you truly returned to your world, what would you do first?
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.ch:
Make sure it hasn't finished rotting.
Then keep moving.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.an:
Ha, you make going home sound like stalking off to kill someone.
Take my glow with you, then. No skulking off in rags.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.an: You've been shattered this badly, and yet somehow you're still... whole?
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.ch: Falling apart and being finished aren't the same thing.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.an:
That is a remarkably beautiful sentence.
Pity you aren't beautiful yourself.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0r.a: If you do go back, at least find yourself something decent to wear first.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1r.c:
First I find a door, then clothes.
Priorities matter.
NONUPEIPE.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2r.a: Exhausting. Fine. Just try not to die looking cheap.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
You look like someone smashed a rainbow and a lightning bolt together.
Weirdly comforting.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.ancie: No head!! Still talking!! Why!? Why!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char:
I'd love to know.
But it is convenient.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.ancie: Convenient good!! Strange also good!! Take item!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.char:
Probably all three.
With some bad temper mixed in.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.ancie: How rift open!? How world split!? Explosion!? Lightning!?
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.char:
If I knew, I wouldn't be here.
All I know is it opened, I chased it, and now there's you.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.ancie: Good!! Tell me next time one opens!! I want go too!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.ancie: Are you stuck to body!? Or body stuck to you!?
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.char:
Good question.
Ask me again next time I come apart.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.ancie: Good!! Show me next time you come apart!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancie: Spire makes people weird!! Your world too!! Very same!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
Yeah.
Difference is mine is wetter, and this one's taller.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.ancie: Tall good!! Wet good!! Both dangerous!! Excellent!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0r.anci: If you find road back, come back and tell story!!
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1r.char: If I come back, it'll probably mean I messed something up again.
OROBAS.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2r.anci: Also good!! Stories about mistakes best stories!!
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
This place smells like damp, flesh, and a nap.
Strangely not unpleasant.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.ancient: [thinky_dots]Tired traveler... there are marks of many deaths upon you...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char:
Mm.
Never really get time to clean them off.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.ancient: [thinky_dots]Then take a part of me... perhaps it will let you last a little longer this time...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.ancien: [thinky_dots]This body... was not the first one either... was it...?[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.char:
Not even close.
The first one's probably rotted past recognition by now.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.ancien: [thinky_dots]The way you shed bodies... reminds me of slipping old skin in sleep...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.ancient: [thinky_dots]You always say you wish to go back... and what then...?[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.char:
Keep dying.
Keep living.
Keep cutting down whatever stands in the way.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.ancient: [thinky_dots]So you belong to neither side... you belong only to 'continue'...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.ancient:
[thinky_dots]You always say 'continue'...
Is it because you no longer remember how stopping feels...?[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.char:
Stopping usually causes problems.
Keeping going's safer.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.ancient: [thinky_dots]Such a sad habit... and such a stubborn one...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancient: [thinky_dots]If you return... will anyone there recognize you...?[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
Probably not.
Then again, I barely recognize the place myself anymore.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.ancient: [thinky_dots]Then perhaps what you seek... is not home... but an exit...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0r.ancien: [thinky_dots]If you are too tired... sit a while... the road will not run away...[/thinky_dots]
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1r.char:
I know.
Trouble always waits where I left it.
PAEL.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2r.ancien: [thinky_dots]At least you have learned a little peace...[/thinky_dots]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
Let's be clear.
I can fight.
But if you yell in my ear again, I'll hit you first.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.ancient: [b]GOOD!! THREATS MEAN SPIRIT!! TAKE A WEAPON!!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char: This place finally has someone reasonable.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.ancien: [b]YOU DIE!! THEN COME BACK!! GOOD!!! THAT MEANS MANY FIGHTS!!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.char: You're the first one who's made that sound like a strength.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.ancien: [b]OF COURSE IT IS!!! FAILING MEANS YOU CAN TRY AGAIN!!! WONDERFUL!!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.char: Swords, bows, whips, ice, fire, traps, frying pans.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancient: [b]YOU LIKE WHAT I LIKE!! FIGHTING!! WEAPONS!! KEEP FIGHTING!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
Close.
I like staying alive.
Fighting just comes with it.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.ancient: [b]STAYING ALIVE MEANS MORE FIGHTING!! SAME THING!!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0r.ancien: [b]WHEN YOU GO BACK!! DO NOT FORGET TO COME BACK AND FIGHT ME AGAIN!!![/b]
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1r.char: If some rift throws me back here again, sure.
TANX.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2r.ancien: [b]GOOD!!! THEN I SHALL WAIT FOR THE RIFT TO BREAK AGAIN!!![/b]
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.ch:
This place smells like dessert, fire, and a little danger.
More interesting than my last restaurant.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.an: My, then your old place doesn't sound very safe either, dear.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.ch:
Safe?
That was the cheapest thing on the whole island.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.an: Then you are perfectly suited to this place. The Spire specializes in roasting danger a little further.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.a: You smell of rot, alchemy, fire... and the kind of arrogance that only grows near a king's table.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-1r.c: You sound like you know my old hellhole.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.a:
Oh, I don't.
Bad ideas just smell the same in every world.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.an: Tell me, dear—who made you into this?
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.ch:
A kingdom.
A plague.
And a pack of people who thought they were clever.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.an:
Mm... I thought so.
The most terrible dishes are always cooked by many fools at once.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.an: Dear, you keep saying 'go back.' But I rather suspect what you truly want is to finish what was started.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-1.ch:
More or less.
If I don't finish it, I don't get back.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.an:
Mm... adorable.
You make obsession sound like a job.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.an: Someone like you is always easiest to trap with the promise of 'one more try.'
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.ch: That's because sometimes one more try actually works.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-2.an: Pity. The Spire is very good at making 'one more try' feel like a gift.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0r.a:
Back already, dear?
What'll it be this time—sweet, spicy, or capable of burning somebody to death?
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1r.c:
Something that points the way would be ideal.
Failing that, deadly will do.
TEZCATARA.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2r.a: Heh. I knew you'd say that.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-0.char:
Let's get one thing clear.
I've had enough kings, monsters, and self-proclaimed problem solvers.
So you'd better get to the point.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-1.ancien:
Direct. Good.
Then I shall be direct as well—I can help you return.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-2.char: When your kind says that, something unpleasant usually follows.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-3.ancien:
A cost, not something unpleasant.
Do not speak of bargains so crudely.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-0r.ancie:
The reason you came here is because the rift recognized you.
It recognized the sort of thing that dies and still refuses to end.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-2r.ancie:
I do not praise.
I appraise.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-0.ancien: If I offered you a contract—to return you to your world, in exchange for all those chances you have to rise again—would you sign?
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-1.char:
No.
I hate being unable to get back.
But I'd hate only getting back once even more.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-2.ancien:
Heh... what a pity.
You won't even trade away your own misfortune.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-0.ancien:
Then keep climbing.
When the Architect dies, the seams of the Spire may loosen.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-2.ancien:
Do not be too pleased.
When the rift opens again, you may not like what waits on the other side.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-0.ancien: Do you really think killing the Architect will guarantee the rift opens again?
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-1.char:
No.
But I prefer cutting down the biggest problem first and seeing what happens after.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-0.ancien:
What I like about you is this: you're like the most valuable kind of clause in a contract.
Unstable, yet endlessly renewable.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-1.char: What I dislike about you is this: you sound like a moldy legal document.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-2.ancien:
Likewise.
You smell like an agreement that's been enforced far too many times.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.6-1r.char:
No.
I'd rather trust luck, violence, and stairs.
VAKUU.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.6-2r.ancie:
A shame.
You really are made to be sold to someone like me.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.0-:
You do not belong to this tower.
You are an external error forced into it.

I am bothered by everything that lies outside the structure.
You most of all.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.1-:
That rift was not my creation.
Neow merely took advantage of it and nailed you into my cycle before it closed.
She figured I'd be good at climbing the Spire over and over.

She was right.
The only difference between you and the other failures here is that you are more accustomed to dying.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.2-:
You want to return.
Then go upward.
If I die, the seams of the tower may split.
You were never kept alive by reassurance.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.3-:
Climb.
Either kill me and see whether the rift reopens,
or die here and become part of the tower.

Fine.
At last you've said something worth hearing.

Then come, foreign monster.
Let me see whether what you honed in another world can leave a mark here.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.4-:
Your world was already a broken machine.
You merely fell from one broken machine into another.
At least the second one talks.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.5-:
You think you seek an exit.
In truth, you are simply better at moving forward inside cages.
Moving forward beats rotting in place.

Perhaps.
The result is usually the same.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.DEADCELLS-BEHEADED.6-:
Foreign monster.
You will find the Spire no kinder than your island.

Good.
I wasn't planning to be kind to it either.

Excellent.
At last, something that understands how to die.
